Kraken describes itself as a Canadian subsea intelligence company with sonar, underwater imaging, subsea power, and robotic systems.
The KATFISH product page describes an actively stabilized towed synthetic aperture sonar system for high-resolution underwater data collection.
Kraken reported purchase orders for synthetic aperture sonar and subsea battery products from defence and commercial customers.
MDA Space presents Earth observation and radar satellite capability as part of its space mission systems portfolio.
MDA CHORUS is described as a next-generation Earth observation constellation using C-band and X-band synthetic aperture radar.
Public contract reporting connects MDA Space services and future CHORUS data use to continuous space-based maritime awareness.
Cellula Robotics presents underwater robotic vehicle capability through its Envoy autonomous underwater vehicle product page.
The Envoy AUV page describes a long-endurance underwater vehicle powered by a fuel cell for extended subsea operation.
Kongsberg Geospatial positions IRIS Terminal around airspace visualization and support for unmanned systems operations.
IRIS Terminal is described as software combining sensor, feed, and telemetry inputs for professional drone and airspace managers.
GeoSpectrum presents underwater acoustic and sonar products for defence and surveillance surface system applications.
TRAPS is described as an active-passive sonar system for detection, classification, tracking, and localization of underwater targets.
Open Ocean Robotics describes autonomous uncrewed surface vehicles for maritime security and ocean data collection.
The company frames its uncrewed vessels as persistent autonomous platforms for ocean monitoring and maritime awareness.
Industry reporting describes Open Ocean Robotics involvement in an autonomous maritime security system demonstration with GSTS.
